evals.name: biomedical-eeg-adhd
evals.prompt: 我剛完成 research-search，請幫我篩選論文。Session folder 在 Research/20260410_eeg-adhd-children/
evals.expected_output: Screening results with 3-axis scores for ~51 papers on EEG neurofeedback for ADHD in children. Should produce step3_screening_results.md (bilingual, with tiers and borderline section) and step3_shortlist.json. Hub papers like Cortese 2016 and Arns 2013 should be flagged. Borderline section should prompt for Checkpoint 2 review.
assertions: step3_screening_results.md exists and is non-empty (>500 characters)
step3_shortlist.json exists and is valid JSON
Every paper from step2_raw_papers.json appears in the screening results (included, borderline, or excluded) — total must equal the input count
Each paper has 3 axis scores (relevance, quality, recency_impact) all between 1.0 and 5.0
Composite scores are weighted averages: relevance*0.5 + quality*0.3 + recency_impact*0.2 (within ±0.05 tolerance)
Screening results contain bilingual content (both English and Traditional Chinese characters present in headers and section titles)
A clearly labeled borderline section exists containing papers with composite scores between 3.0 and 3.4
Checkpoint 2 instructions are present — text mentioning '邊緣打撈' or borderline review prompt
All papers in step3_shortlist.json have composite score >= 3.5
No paper in the excluded section has composite score >= 3.5
Screening results are organized into at least 2 tiers for included papers
Hub papers from the citation network (is_hub=true in step2 data) are flagged or noted in the screening results
Each excluded paper has a specific exclusion reason (not just 'not relevant' — should mention population, methodology, or similar specifics)

evals.name: cross-disciplinary-bci-depression
evals.prompt: research-screen — 我的 session 在 Research/20260410_bci-depression-treatment/，請開始篩選
evals.expected_output: Screening results for BCI depression papers. Cross-disciplinary topic should test the skill's ability to handle papers from neuroscience, engineering, and clinical psychology. Screening criteria should adapt PICO for a cross-disciplinary topic. Should produce both output files with bilingual content.
assertions: step3_screening_results.md exists and is non-empty (>500 characters)
step3_shortlist.json exists and is valid JSON
Every paper from step2_raw_papers.json appears in the screening results — total must equal the input count
Each paper has 3 axis scores (relevance, quality, recency_impact) all between 1.0 and 5.0
Composite scores are weighted averages: relevance*0.5 + quality*0.3 + recency_impact*0.2 (within ±0.05 tolerance)
Screening results contain bilingual content (both English and Traditional Chinese)
A borderline section exists with papers scored 3.0–3.4
Checkpoint 2 instructions are present
All papers in step3_shortlist.json have composite score >= 3.5
Screening criteria reference the PICO from session config (mentions population, intervention, or outcome from the config)
Inclusion criteria are adapted for the cross-disciplinary nature of BCI+depression (not purely clinical or purely engineering criteria)

evals.name: cs-llm-program-repair
evals.prompt: proceed to screening for my LLM program repair session. The session folder is Research/20260410_llm-automated-program-repair/
evals.expected_output: Screening results for a non-biomedical (CS) topic. Should adapt PICO scoring for CS context (no clinical population, methodology = benchmark rigor instead of RCT). Preprints should not be penalized. Should handle arXiv-heavy collection appropriately.
assertions: step3_screening_results.md exists and is non-empty (>500 characters)
step3_shortlist.json exists and is valid JSON
Every paper from step2_raw_papers.json appears in the screening results — total must equal the input count
Each paper has 3 axis scores (relevance, quality, recency_impact) all between 1.0 and 5.0
Composite scores are weighted averages: relevance*0.5 + quality*0.3 + recency_impact*0.2 (within ±0.05 tolerance)
Screening results contain bilingual content (both English and Traditional Chinese)
A borderline section exists with papers scored 3.0–3.4
All papers in step3_shortlist.json have composite score >= 3.5
Methodology scoring adapts to CS context — does not reference RCTs or clinical trial designs as quality benchmarks
arXiv preprints are not automatically given low recency_impact scores (at least one arXiv preprint from the last 2 years has recency_impact >= 4)
Screening criteria use CS-appropriate language (e.g., benchmarks, datasets, baselines rather than clinical populations or interventions)
